defs: The act of making satisfaction or atonement for any crime or fault; the extinguishing of guilt by suffering or penalty.
quotes: His liberality seemed to have something in it of self-abasement and expiation.

defs: The means by which reparation or atonement for crimes or sins is made; an expiatory sacrifice or offering; an atonement.
quotes: Those shadowy expiations weak, The blood of bulls and goats.

defs: An act by which the threats of prodigies were averted among the ancient heathen.
